origin.name: Georgia
origin.key_document: Death certificate from Civil Registry Agency (requires certified English translation)
origin.source: FCDO Georgia travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Tbilisi guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Georgia is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is an option alongside full body repatriation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Death certificate from Civil Registry Agency (certified English translation required)
special_rules: Death must be registered with the Civil Registry Agency promptly.
All Georgian-language documentation requires a certified English translation for UK acceptance.
The Prosecutor's Office orders post-mortem for suspicious or violent deaths.
An export permit from the relevant Georgian authority is required for all repatriations.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Georgian documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Tbilisi can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Georgia to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Georgia. Repatriation to the UK takes 1-2 weeks. Civil Registry Agency, Georgian translation, and export permits all handled. Contact us 24/7.
